question:
Which of the following books are related to 'political economy'?
A. Hans Morgenthau – Politics Among Nations
B. Adam Smith – Wealth of Nations
C. David Ricardo – Principles of Political Economy and Taxation
D. Karl Marx – Economic and Philosophical Manuscripts
E. J.S. Mill – Principles of Political Economy

Choose the correct answer:
options: A, B, C and D only
B, C, D and E only
A, C, D and E only
A, B, D and E only
explanation: B, C, D and E are works in political economy. Morgenthau's 'Politics Among Nations' (A) is a foundational text of International Relations / realism, not political economy.

question:
Arrange the formation of the following Inter-State Water Dispute Tribunals in the correct chronological order (earliest first):
A. Godavari Water Dispute Tribunal
B. Mahanadi Water Dispute Tribunal
C. Ravi and Beas Water Dispute Tribunal
D. Cauvery Water Dispute Tribunal
E. Mahadayi Water Dispute Tribunal
explanation: Year of constitution: Godavari (1969), Ravi & Beas (1986), Cauvery (1990), Mahadayi (2010), Mahanadi (2018) — i.e. A, C, D, E, B.

question: The Minnowbrook-I Conference, associated with the rise of the New Public Administration, was held in:
explanation: The first Minnowbrook Conference was held in 1968 under Dwight Waldo; it gave rise to the New Public Administration movement emphasising social equity and relevance.

question: Who wrote the book 'The Practice of Management' (1954), which introduced the concept of Management by Objectives (MBO)?
explanation: Peter Drucker's 'The Practice of Management' (1954) introduced Management by Objectives (MBO).

question:
Match List-I (Concept/Approach) with List-II (Proposer):

List-I
A. Human Relations approach to organisations
B. Bounded Rationality
C. Contribution-satisfaction equilibrium
D. Self-actualisation

List-II
I. Chester Barnard
II. Abraham Maslow
III. Herbert Simon
IV. George Elton Mayo
explanation: Elton Mayo – Human Relations approach; Herbert Simon – Bounded Rationality; Chester Barnard – contribution-satisfaction equilibrium (inducements-contributions); Abraham Maslow – self-actualisation. So A-IV, B-III, C-I, D-II.

question: The book 'Political Parties: A Sociological Study of the Oligarchical Tendencies of Modern Democracy' was written by:
explanation: Robert Michels' 'Political Parties' (1911) advanced the 'iron law of oligarchy' — the thesis that all organisations, even democratic ones, tend to become oligarchic.

question:
Match List-I (Political Event) with List-II (Year):

List-I
A. Dismissal of the Namboodiripad Government (Kerala)
B. Operation Blue Star
C. M.M. Punchhi Commission Report
D. Sarkaria Commission

List-II
I. 1987-88
II. 1984
III. 2010
IV. 1959
explanation: The first elected Communist (Namboodiripad) government in Kerala was dismissed in 1959; Operation Blue Star was in 1984; the Sarkaria Commission on Centre-State relations reported in 1987-88; the Punchhi Commission reported in 2010. So A-IV, B-II, C-III, D-I.
